items.title: As AI gets more capable, product teams should spend more time on external communication (talkin…
items.content: As AI gets more capable, product teams should spend more time on external communication (talking to users, customers, etc) than internal communication 1. Figuring out “what” to build will be a lot more important than building it. Getting this right requires sparks of inspiration that strike when you talk to external people, esp those who are every different from you. Since AI is very good at implementing solutions, humans should focus on understanding the PROBLEM 2. Teams will be a lot smaller,…

items.title: I think “play” should be taken more seriously If you work in AI, playing with the models is lit…
items.content: I think “play” should be taken more seriously If you work in AI, playing with the models is literally part of your job By play I mean “testing the models’ limits in a non-utilitarian way, with a sense of playful exploration and experimentation” Not only is it NOT a waste of time, but I often find it to be the most productive part of my day when I look back

items.title: How I came up with this idea: 1. I first realized all Chrome browsing history is saved locally…
items.content: How I came up with this idea: 1. I first realized all Chrome browsing history is saved locally in a database and can be parsed by LLM; so I was like: what can I build with this? 2. I tried to build an extension that surfaces insights based on browser history. But I realized if I had to click a button to bring it up every time, I'm never gonna click it. I need an entry-point that I'm already visiting frequently 3. I asked Claude, can you turn Chrome's New Tab page into the entry point? To my sur…

items.content: Ok I have simplified Tab Out even further: - Tab Out is now a pure Chrome extension. No server, no Node.js, no npm - Saved tabs are stored in https://t.co/ggaSm4eBVR.local; 100% local For this little app I spent 20% of the time building features and 80% of the time cutting features. Claude tends to overkill; you need to keep it in check

items.title: PSA: You can vibe code your own "New tab" page in Chrome. I have turned mine into the ultimate…
items.content: PSA: You can vibe code your own "New tab" page in Chrome. I have turned mine into the ultimate solution to the "too many tabs" problem - See all your tabs with clear titles, grouped by domain - Closing any tab gives you "swoosh" sound and confetti effect 🎊 - "Easy wins" grouped together: homepages, localhost tabs... batch-close them with one click - Duplicate tabs detected; close duplicates with one click - For tabs you're not done with, save it for later in a checklist This is the Marie Kondo…

items.title: Don't just ask AI to "summarize" a piece of long-form/difficult content. Try these prompts: 1.…
items.content: Don't just ask AI to "summarize" a piece of long-form/difficult content. Try these prompts: 1. Remix it into a polished magazine article, preserving the best original quotes 2. Remix it into a Socratic dialogue between a teacher & a student (representing me) 3. Based on what you know about me, pick out the insights that I would find the most interesting

items.title: I seldom use AI for writing because I actually enjoy writing and find it very easy & fun to do…
items.content: I seldom use AI for writing because I actually enjoy writing and find it very easy & fun to do And because I have a high bar for writing, if I use AI to write, it almost always ends up taking more time than if I had written it by myself (too many iterations) For me personally, AI has been most useful for helping me with things OUTSIDE my comfort zone (like coding), not inside of it (like writing)
